attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a wet bar of soap into the white cabinet.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene depicts a bathroom vanity area with the following relevant objects and their properties:

1.  **Objects and their types:**
    -   **Receptacles:**
        -   **Cabinet:** A white, double-door cabinet unit positioned beneath the countertop. It is openable. Each door has a small, dark, vertical handle. The cabinet is currently closed. It is a receptacle capable of holding objects.
        -   **Sink:** A dark gray, rectangular vessel sink embedded in a light wood-grain countertop. It is a receptacle and is the designated location for cleaning objects. It is currently empty.
    -   **Objects:**
        -   **Bar of Soap:** A small, rectangular, pink bar of soap. It is currently resting on the countertop, to the right of the left sink basin.
        -   **Faucets:** Two ornate, bronze-colored faucets are mounted on the countertop, one for each sink basin. They are not receptacles.
        -   **Toilet:** A white toilet is visible to the right of the vanity. It is not a receptacle as per the domain definition.
        -   **Mirror:** A large mirror is mounted on the wall above the sinks. It is not a receptacle.
        -   **Candlestick:** A decorative gold candlestick with a white candle is on top of the toilet tank. It is not a receptacle.
        -   **Toilet Paper:** A roll of toilet paper is mounted on the wall next to the toilet. It is not a receptacle.

2.  **Quantities:**
    -   1 Cabinet (receptacle)
    -   2 Sinks (receptacles)
    -   1 Bar of Soap (object)
    -   2 Faucets (objects)
    -   1 Toilet (object)
    -   1 Mirror (object)
    -   1 Candlestick (object)
    -   1 Toilet Paper Roll (object)

3.  **Spatial Relationships:**
    -   The **Cabinet** is located directly beneath the **Countertop**.
    -   The **Sink** is embedded within the **Countertop**.
    -   The **Bar of Soap** is on the **Countertop**, adjacent to the left **Sink**.
    -   The **Faucets** are mounted on the **Countertop**, positioned over each **Sink**.
    -   The **Toilet** is to the right of the **Cabinet** and **Countertop** unit.
    -   The **Mirror** is mounted on the wall above the **Countertop** and **Sinks**.
    -   The **Candlestick** is on top of the **Toilet** tank.
    -   The **Toilet Paper** is on the wall next to the **Toilet**.

4.  **Current State of Objects (Relevant to Task):**
    -   The **Cabinet** is closed (`not (opened ?r)`).
    -   The **Sink** is empty and ready for use.
    -   The **Bar of Soap** is dry and has not been cleaned (`not (isClean ?o)`). It is not currently held by any agent.
    -   The **Faucets** are off (`not (isOn ?o)`).

5.  **Visual Properties:**
    -   **Cabinet:** White, matte finish, two doors with dark vertical handles.
    -   **Sink:** Dark gray, matte, rectangular vessel style.
    -   **Countertop:** Light wood grain, flat surface.
    -   **Bar of Soap:** Small, rectangular, pink.
    -   **Faucets:** Bronze, ornate, cross-handle design.
    -   **Floor:** Black and white checkered tile.
    -   **Wall:** Light gray, textured finish.
    -   **Toilet:** Standard white ceramic.
    -   **Mirror:** Reflective surface, showing a partial reflection of the room.
    -   **Candlestick:** Gold-colored metal with a white candle.
    -   **Toilet Paper:** Standard white roll.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_wet_soap_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        cabinet - receptacle
        sink - receptacle
        bar_of_soap - object
        faucet1 faucet2 - object
        toilet mirror candlestick toilet_paper - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (atLocation agent1 cabinet)
        (atLocation agent1 sink)
        (atLocation agent1 bar_of_soap)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean bar_of_soap))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle bar_of_soap cabinet)
        (isClean bar_of_soap)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_wet_soap_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        cabinet - receptacle
        sink - receptacle
        bar_of_soap - object
        faucet1 faucet2 - object
        toilet mirror candlestick toilet_paper - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (atLocation agent1 cabinet)
        (atLocation agent1 sink)
        (atLocation agent1 bar_of_soap)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean bar_of_soap))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle bar_of_soap cabinet)
        (isClean bar_of_soap)
    ))
)

        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a wet bar of soap into the white cabinet.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts a bathroom vanity area with multiple objects relevant to the planning task. The primary objects of interest are the sink, the cabinet (which functions as a receptacle), and a bar of soap.

1. Objects and their types:
   - Sink: There are two identical dark gray rectangular vessel sinks mounted on a light brown countertop. Each sink is equipped with a bronze-colored faucet. According to the domain, a sink is a receptacle type and is capable of cleaning objects.
   - Cabinet: Below the countertop, there is a white cabinet with two doors on the left side and two doors on the right side, totaling four cabinet doors. The cabinet is a receptacle and is openable. The doors have small black handles.
   - Bar of soap: A small pink rectangular bar of soap is visible on the countertop, positioned between the left sink and the toilet. This is an object (not a receptacle) and is the target item to be put into the cabinet.
   - Toilet: A white toilet is visible to the right of the sink area. It is not a receptacle in the domain and is not relevant to the task.
   - Mirror: A large mirror is mounted on the wall above the sinks, reflecting parts of the room. It is not a receptacle and not relevant to the task.
   - Floor: The floor is tiled with a checkerboard pattern of dark gray and light beige tiles. It is not a receptacle.

2. Quantities of each object type:
   - Sink: 2
   - Cabinet: 1 (with 4 doors, but treated as a single receptacle unit)
   - Bar of soap: 1
   - Toilet: 1
   - Mirror: 1

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The two sinks are side by side on the countertop.
   - The cabinet is directly below the countertop and spans the width of both sinks.
   - The bar of soap is located on the countertop, to the right of the left sink and to the left of the toilet.
   - The toilet is to the right of the right sink.
   - The mirror is above the sinks and cabinet.
   - The floor is beneath all objects.

4. Current state of each object relevant to the planning task:
   - The cabinet is currently closed (not opened).
   - The bar of soap is currently on the countertop (not in any receptacle and not held by the agent).
   - The sinks are not currently being used for cleaning (no object is being cleaned in them).
   - The agent is not at any object’s location initially.

5. Colors, positions, and other visual properties:
   - The sinks are dark gray with bronze faucets.
   - The countertop is light brown.
   - The cabinet is white with black handles.
   - The bar of soap is pink and rectangular.
   - The toilet is white.
   - The mirror has a reflective surface.
   - The floor has a checkerboard pattern with alternating dark gray and light beige tiles.

The task requires the agent to first go to the bar of soap, pick it up, clean it in one of the sinks (to make it wet), then go to the cabinet, open it, and put the wet soap inside. The cabinet must be opened before the soap can be placed inside.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_wet_soap_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 sink2 - sink
        cabinet - receptacle
        soap - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (not (atLocation agent1 soap))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean soap))
        (not (inReceptacle soap cabinet))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle soap cabinet)
        (isClean soap)
    ))
)
```

(gotolocation agent1 soap)
(pickupobject agent1 soap)
(gotolocation agent1 sink1)
(cleanobject agent1 sink1 soap)
(gotolocation agent1 cabinet)
(putobjectinreceptacle agent1 soap cabinet)

Scene Description:

The scene depicts a bathroom vanity area with multiple objects relevant to the planning task. The primary objects of interest are the sink, the cabinet (which functions as a receptacle), and a bar of soap.

1. Objects and their types:
   - Sink: There are two identical dark gray rectangular vessel sinks mounted on a light brown countertop. Each sink is equipped with a bronze-colored faucet. According to the domain, a sink is a receptacle type and is capable of cleaning objects.
   - Cabinet: Below the countertop, there is a white cabinet with two doors on the left side and two doors on the right side, totaling four cabinet doors. The cabinet is a receptacle and is openable. The doors have small black handles.
   - Bar of soap: A small pink rectangular bar of soap is visible on the countertop, positioned between the left sink and the toilet. This is an object (not a receptacle) and is the target item to be put into the cabinet.
   - Toilet: A white toilet is visible to the right of the sink area. It is not a receptacle in the domain and is not relevant to the task.
   - Mirror: A large mirror is mounted on the wall above the sinks, reflecting parts of the room. It is not a receptacle and not relevant to the task.
   - Floor: The floor is tiled with a checkerboard pattern of dark gray and light beige tiles. It is not a receptacle.

2. Quantities of each object type:
   - Sink: 2
   - Cabinet: 1 (with 4 doors, but treated as a single receptacle unit)
   - Bar of soap: 1
   - Toilet: 1
   - Mirror: 1

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The two sinks are side by side on the countertop.
   - The cabinet is directly below the countertop and spans the width of both sinks.
   - The bar of soap is located on the countertop, to the right of the left sink and to the left of the toilet.
   - The toilet is to the right of the right sink.
   - The mirror is above the sinks and cabinet.
   - The floor is beneath all objects.

4. Current state of each object relevant to the planning task:
   - The cabinet is currently closed (not opened).
   - The bar of soap is currently on the countertop (not in any receptacle and not held by the agent).
   - The sinks are not currently being used for cleaning (no object is being cleaned in them).
   - The agent is not at any object’s location initially.

5. Colors, positions, and other visual properties:
   - The sinks are dark gray with bronze faucets.
   - The countertop is light brown.
   - The cabinet is white with black handles.
   - The bar of soap is pink and rectangular.
   - The toilet is white.
   - The mirror has a reflective surface.
   - The floor has a checkerboard pattern with alternating dark gray and light beige tiles.

The task requires the agent to first go to the bar of soap, pick it up, clean it in one of the sinks (to make it wet), then go to the cabinet, open it, and put the wet soap inside. The cabinet must be opened before the soap can be placed inside.

Generate the PDDL problem:
final.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_wet_soap_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 sink2 - sink
        cabinet - receptacle
        soap - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (not (atLocation agent1 soap))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean soap))
        (not (inReceptacle soap cabinet))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle soap cabinet)
        (isClean soap)
    ))
)
```
final.problem:
(define (problem put_wet_soap_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 sink2 - sink
        cabinet - receptacle
        soap - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (not (atLocation agent1 soap))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean soap))
        (not (inReceptacle soap cabinet))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle soap cabinet)
        (isClean soap)
    ))
)
